mechanism_narrative: Growth in retail sales signals expanding consumer demand and corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Retail sales growth coincides with negative 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 45 percent of months over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate above 8.0 percent indicates severe labor market slack that suppresses wage income and consumer spending, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 8.0 percent, positive 60-day S&P 500 returns occur more than 50 percent of the time over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates combined with rising housing starts transmit monetary stimulus into real economic activity through construction and employment multipliers, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25% and HOUST is rising month-over-month, positive 20-day returns occur less than 55% of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth during post-recession recovery signals rising capacity utilization and operating leverage, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: During post-recession recovery, positive 5-day returns occur less than 55 percent of the time following industrial production growth over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint growth in retail sales and payrolls confirms demand expansion is employment-supported rather than credit-fueled, generating sustained positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint growth in RSAFS and PAYEMS coincides with negative 60-day returns in more than 40 percent of instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.25 percent forces investors out the risk curve into equities due to lack of yield, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS below 0.25 percent, 20-day forward returns are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05) over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A three-month decline in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market normalization, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Following a 0.2pp decline in UNRATE over 3 months, positive 5-day returns occur less than 55 percent of the time over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Increases in housing starts signal construction sector momentum and employment multiplier effects, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: When HOUST is rising month-over-month, 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 45 percent of quarters over any 3-year rolling period.
